Step: User shares data source and indicates: feedback column · unique ID column · # of rows to analyse
Notes: Paste a local CSV path + column names + row count in the chat. No files written.

Tech: Python (pipeline.prepare_output) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Reads source CSV · filters blank quotes · takes first N rows (no shuffle) · writes output.csv with columns: row_number · id · quote · tag (empty). File written: output.csv → /project/output.csv.

Tech: Python (pipeline.print_unique_tags) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Reads approved-records.csv and prints all unique tags. If file does not exist or is empty prints 'no existing tags'. No files written.
Claude asks the user for any guidance: rename a tag everywhere · remove a tag everywhere · add a new tag · tips on tag naming format or level of detail. User responds in chat.

Tech: Python (pipeline.apply_tag_changes) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Applies any renames or removals from step 4 to both approved-records.csv and output.csv (if they exist). Files updated: approved-records.csv · output.csv.

Tech: Python (pipeline.build_reference_tags) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Reads approved-records.csv · takes 1–3 example quotes per tag · adds instructions from step 4. File written: reference-tags.json. Structure: { instructions: [string ...] · tags: [{ tag · examples }] }.

Tech: Python (pipeline.apply_pretags) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Matches each row in output.csv to approved-records.csv by unique ID. Where IDs match copies the existing tag into output.csv. File updated: output.csv. Prints count of pre-tagged rows.

Step: AI assigns tags to remaining untagged rows
Tech: Claude AI (in chat — no API key needed)
Notes: Claude calls get_untagged_rows() · reads reference-tags.json · assigns a tag to each quote using instructions and examples as reference · creates new tags when no existing tag fits · calls save_tags() to write results. File updated: output.csv.

Tech: Python (pipeline.print_tag_summary) — run by Claude in chat
Notes: Prints unique tag list and occurrence counts from output.csv. No files written.

Step: Tell user to refresh the app
Notes: Claude tells the user the analysis is complete and to refresh the browser. No files written.
Start server once: uvicorn server:app --reload → open http://localhost:8000. App reads output.csv via GET /api/data. Left pane: unique tag list. Right pane: table (row_number · id · quote · tag).

Step: User edits tags in the right pane
Notes: Edit any tag cell inline. New tags sync to the left-pane list automatically. No files written until step 15 or 16.

Step: User edits tags in the left pane
Notes: Rename or remove a tag in the textarea then click Apply — cascades to all matching rows instantly. No files written until step 15 or 16.
Row range filter (from / to row_number) and tag filter work independently or together. Useful for reviewing only newly added rows. No files written.
User clicks Save to approved-records.csv. File overwritten: approved-records.csv (columns: id · quote · tag).
User clicks Save & download output.csv. File overwritten: output.csv (columns: row_number · id · quote · tag) then browser download triggered.
User clicks Clear all files. Files deleted: output.csv · approved-records.csv · reference-tags.json. App returns to waiting screen.
